title: Silicone Jar Grip (2)
description:
About this item
Your secret weapon when it comes to opening jars and lids. Open with ease!
Food Grade approved non-slip silicone helps prevent any sliding while opening jars!
4.8in Diameter
Durable, lightweight, machine or hand-washable.
Doubles as a potholder or trivet.
› See more product details
reviews_5s:
It is a simple device that greatly aids in removing jar lid, but hard to find in brick and mortar stores.I have severe arthritis and hate constantly asking others to open things for me. These work the BEST! I have them everywhere - kitchen (in two drawers), sewing room, car, drawer at work plus my husband took one for his truck. Gave one to my daughter for opening stubborn jars. My grand daughter uses them too - knows exactly which kitchen drawer it’s in. Co-workers borrow mine all the time. Just bought 2 more - 1 for my OT hand therapist for her goody bag she uses to show patients the tools that are available to help them and one to have as an extra since my husband took the extra I had. 😊Great grippers! Serves the purpose well with opening caps & jars.WORKS AS LONG AS I REMEMBER WHERE I PUT EMThe jar wrestler works great for opening any tight lid. It's durable, sturdy and good value. I bought 1 to see how it works but you might want to buy more. Sometimes you need to get a grip at the bottom as well as the top to get jars and etc open. 👍After my kids broke the handle on the bathtub faucet for the third (!!!) time, I decided to purchase this item rather than spend the money to replace the entire mechanism. To turn the water on without a handle requires a good, strong grip; without such a grip, the remaining piece of the faucet thingy will not work. This item is a little sticky and very grippy, so my children and I can turn the water on despite the missing faucet handle. Eventually, I will spend bigger money to replace the faucet. Until then, the small money I spent on this Jar (faucet?) Wrestler was worth it.I have very rambunctious dogs who bug the crap out of me when I'm trying to do work on my laptop because it means they cannot get my attention so I started giving them frozen baby food jars and it worked wonders! Except it also worked the bad kind of wonders on my hands trying to get the darn things open especially when I make my own concoctions.

I didn't want to pay more than $1 for a jar gripper but after hurting my hand trying to open a jar I compromised and paid the $4.99 price and am SO HAPPY I did!

Pros:
-it works VERY well!
-it's the perfect size where it is not difficult to store and not too small to be useable.
-decent design with the colors (hate boring tans and whites)
-my hands don't hurt anymore and my dogs are happy so all in all great!

Cons:
-I'm kind of cheap so I would prefer it to be $1 but honestly I think that about everything so make of that what you willNice compact size! I work as a flight attendant and purchased the Jar Wrestler to help with opening liquor mini's. In the past I have used a silicone hot pad which worked perfectly well but was too big to fit easily in my pocket. This is a good size and can be shoved into a pocket without issue. It is a little more sticky that I am accustomed to. The material is very thin which can lead to your hand hurting if you are exerting a lot of squeeze on the item you are attempting to open, but in the long run this little item saves my hand from being rubbed raw at the end of the day. Completely worth it!I have rheumatoid arthritis, sometimes it’s impossible for me to open jars, bottles, just about anything.. These really help so much. I haven’t had to ask my husband to open anything since I got these!It is so hard just to find a THIN gripper for jars. Most products these days are too thick but this is exactly what I was looking for.
reviews_1s:
I wanted this product to open jars. The color is beautiful. I dislike its ability to open jars as it was so advertised. I struggle still with opening a jar using this product for a grip to open. I have had previous products for that purpose(to open jars) that was great. I just forgot where it was purchased.
This product on a scale of 1 to 10 with 10 being the best, scores a 2 in my opinion.Very disappointed. Thought this would help me with stubborn jar lid's. Can't even get a grip. There are better options out there.
This even feels cheapWill be returning this product. It works fine, but the Chemical smell is nauseous & toxic. They are in individual sealed plastic bags. I took them out of the plastic bags & aired them for three days. I still can't stand the smell. I don't know what type of chemical was used to make these Jar Grips, but the product manager should check into this.Thinner silicone easily opens jars. This one is too thick and one side is scored. Had a hard time opening jars.These did not grip enough. My hands don't work well, thanks to Rheumatoid and I need help opening jars. I was hoping these would but I was sadly disappointed. Waste of moneyThis is definitely not worth the price. It is not wide enough to do the jars you have the most difficult with....very disappointing. I have arthritis and was hoping to get a product that would help me. Don't waste your money.While this item does help somewhat, it failed on many of the things that I needed to open.It not open a jar I was having
Difficulty withThis product Did Not do as stated-it Does Not Grip the top of any jar that l tried to open! I do not endorse this product 🤨Does not work as advertised
